page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 32 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, along with hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI cryptographic instructions. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, its multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the physical core count limitation. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak. Cryptographic and text processing tasks perform at average levels, whereas database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving benchmarks show weaker relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highmem-4 offers a high memory density per dedicated vCPU without the premium associated with larger instance sizes. This makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. Recommended workloads include small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-intensive text processing, and lightweight single-threaded applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and database operations fall into the lower performance tier. Large-block memory bandwidth and text processing remain average. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications with moderate parallel processing needs, making it suitable for small databases, caching layers, and single-threaded memory-bound workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 features 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound tasks
